sections.paragraph:
fut. יִצֹּק, pl. יִעְקוּ (once fut. E. וַיֵּצֶק 1Ki 22:35 [in some copies], and there intransitive), imp. צַק 2Ki 4:41, and יְצֹק Eze 24:3 inf. צֶקֶת.

(1) to pour, to pour out, liquids, Gen 28:18 35:14 Exo 29:7 2Ki 4:4 to cast metal, such as brass, Exo 25:12 26:37 36:36 . Part. pass. יָצוּק cast, 1Ki 7:24, 30 1Ki 7:30hence hard, firm, like cast metal, Job 41:15, 16 Job 41:16. Metaph. Psa 41:9, דְּבַר בְּלִיַּעַל יָצוּק בֹּו “a wicked purpose is poured out upon him.”

(2) to pour self out, to flow out, 1Ki 22:35 Job 38:38, בְּצֶקֶת עָפָר לַמּוּצָק “where dust flows into a molten mass,” i.e. it becomes wet with rain water, like a molten mass.

Piel, to pour out. Part. מְיַצֶּקֶת 2Ki 4:5 כתיב.

Hiphil הֹוצִיק id. 2 Ki. loc. cit. קרי, but in another form-

Hiphil הִצִּיק is to place, to set, i.q. הִצִּיג (the ideas of pouring, casting, placing, being connected), Jos 7:23 2Sa 15:24.

Hophal הוּצַק.-

(1) to be poured out, Lev 21:10 Psa 45:3.

(2) to be cast from metal, 1Ki 7:23, 33 1Ki 7:33 Job 37:18. Metaph. מֻצָּק firm, fearless, Job 11:15.

Derivatives, מוּצָק, מוּצָקָה [מֻצֶקֶת], and-
